channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Screen Australia hands out $1.4 million to local developers
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 24, 2026

1 Min Read

Newsbrief: Screen Australia has invested $1.4 million into 26 video game projects crafted by local studios and teams. The government-backed agency said the cash will support the creation of original titles "centred on Australian stories and ideas." 

The financing was handed out through the Games Production and Emerging Gamemakers funds. Recipients include Decaying Salvage developer Stasis Booth Games, Little Ruin maker Lucernal, and Delverium creator Sagestone Games. 

Screen Australia head of games, Joey Egger, said supporting local game creators remains an "essential" part of the agency's remit. 

"Having Australian funding support for games is essential for building a resilient and innovative games sector for the next five, ten, twenty years," he added. "It’s also inspiring to see creators from diverse backgrounds like architecture, animation and the performing arts working hand-in-hand with some of our more experienced gamemakers."

Related:Capcom says it will use generative AI to speed up production

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/screen-australia-hands-out-1-4-million-to-local-developers
